name: Artemis II
first: First crewed flight beyond low-Earth orbit since Apollo 17 (1972) — and the mission that inspired this project
description: The Artemis program's first crewed flight, and the spark that became Orrery. NASA's SLS Block 1 launched an Orion spacecraft with four astronauts — Reid Wiseman (CDR), Victor Glover (PLT), Christina Koch (MS), and CSA's Jeremy Hansen (MS) — onto a hybrid free-return trajectory that swung them around the Moon's far side at roughly 9,200 km altitude and returned them to Earth without any lunar-orbit insertion burn. A 10-day mission ending with Pacific splashdown. Qualified Orion's life-support, navigation, and re-entry systems for crewed flight ahead of the Artemis III surface landing.

events.label: SLS LAUNCH
events.note: Orion lifts off from KSC LC-39B with four crew aboard.

events.label: TLI
events.note: Trans-lunar injection burn by ICPS — Orion separates onto cislunar trajectory.

events.label: LUNAR FLYBY
events.note: Closest approach over the far side, ~9,200 km altitude. No orbit insertion — free-return geometry slings Orion back to Earth.

events.label: SPLASHDOWN
events.note: Orion re-entry and Pacific Ocean splashdown.
